title: Eupatorium kingii
taxonomy: Eupatorium kingii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Eupatorium, which consists of around 40-50 species of flowering plants. The genus Eupatorium is further divided into two subgenera, Eupatorium and Junella. Eupatorium kingii is native to North America and is commonly known as the Gray Donald or Gray Donald's thoroughwort. The classification of Eupatorium kingii reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants in the Asteraceae family.
uses: Eupatorium kingii, also known as Gravel Root, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits in treating urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and gravel, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant are said to contain compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and soothe urinary tract discomfort. Traditionally, Gravel Root has been used as a diuretic to help promote urine production and relieve symptoms associated with urinary tract infections. Additionally, some herbal remedies claim that Gravel Root may help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other gastrointestinal issues. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Eupatorium kingii in treating these conditions.

properties: Eupatorium kingii, also known as Gravel Root, has been used in traditional medicine for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots are said to have a cooling effect on the body, making it useful for treating fever and heat-related illnesses. Eupatorium kingii is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and promote wound healing. constituents: Eupatorium kingii, also known as Gray's blazing star, is a plant species that contains various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in the plant, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The phenolic acids, including caffeic and ferulic acids, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-oxidative properties. The essential oils in Gray's blazing star have been reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
parts: Eupatorium kingii, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its leaves and roots. The leaves are used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever and pain. preparations: Eupatorium kingii is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations are primarily focused on addressing various health issues.
